creator: bofh@freebsd.org
text:
I am not sure about what you mean by DOCS is selected? I thought DOCS, EXAMPLES and NLS are by default on. 
And in fact poudriere logs also say that :
---Begin OPTIONS List---
===> The following configuration options are available for freetds-devel-0.92.812,1:
     DOCS=on: Build and/or install documentation
     ODBCWIDE=off: Enable Wide ODBC Support

And if I build with -i option in poudriere and check it out:
root@110amd64-default:~ # find /wrkdirs/usr/ports/databases/freetds-devel/work/ | grep refer
/wrkdirs/usr/ports/databases/freetds-devel/work/freetds-dev.0.92.812/doc/reference
/wrkdirs/usr/ports/databases/freetds-devel/work/freetds-dev.0.92.812/doc/reference/index.html
/wrkdirs/usr/ports/databases/freetds-devel/work/stage/usr/local/share/doc/freetds/reference
/wrkdirs/usr/ports/databases/freetds-devel/work/stage/usr/local/share/doc/freetds/reference/index.html

(In reply to Kurt Jaeger from comment #2)
> I tested it, works in the default mode.
> 
> If DOCS is selected, it fails, see
> 
> http://people.freebsd.org/~pi/logs/freetds-with-DOC.txt
> 
> Can you investigate this ?
